The order of adjectives can also vary. This means order typically follows from larger to smaller based on these categories for descriptive purposes.
(B) terrible + archaic + tan + Vietnamese + walking + baby, correct. The word terrible conveys quality, and it comes first. The word archaic (quality) appears next. Words tan (color) and Vietnamese (nationality/geographic origin) follow.  The word walking (form/ shape) is described as an adjective that helps to describe baby.  Note: The normal order of the modifiers is size or any modifiers that imply quantity, quality, a descriptive noun if appropriate, a word that indicates the person’s/thing’s type.  So, the sequence typically starts with quality, then a descriptive noun for the thing in question. Next is the origin of the thing (notice: this comes before the general category), and so forth.
(C) Removing order: terrible, archaic, Vietnamese, tan, walking, baby
(D) terrible, walking does not make sense as quality and form have the adjectives interchanged.
